name: ORG-CONTEXT Ontology (Context Hub)
description: Rich organizational context hub bridging ORG entities to domain-specific ontologies. Links products, brands, competitive landscape, market segments, maturity assessment, design systems, and authority domains via the context hub pattern. Includes absorbed entities from CL-ONT and CA-ONT.

components.OrganizationContext: Central hub entity linking all contextual sub-entities for an organization
components.Product: Product or service offered by the organization to target market segments
components.Brand: Brand owned by the organization carrying its own market identity
components.ProductPortfolio: Structured view of complete product and service offerings
components.CompetitiveLandscape: Competitive environment with competitors, forces, and trends
components.Competitor: Named competitor organization with competitive analysis
components.CompetitiveForce: Porter-style competitive force analysing structural market pressure
components.Trend: Macro trend affecting the market landscape (PESTEL)
components.MarketContext: Market segments, geographic scope, and customer needs
components.MarketSegment: Target market segment defined by industry, geography, and priority
components.CustomerNeed: High-level customer need: jobs-to-be-done, pain points, value drivers
components.OrganizationMaturity: Multi-dimensional maturity assessment across size, value, marketing, tech, AI
components.CompetitorProfile: Detailed competitor intelligence profile with market share, strengths, weaknesses. Absorbed from CL-ONT.
components.MarketDynamic: Market shift, disruption, or emerging pattern affecting competitive landscape. Absorbed from CL-ONT.
components.CompetitiveAdvantage: Sustainable competitive advantage held by the organization or a competitor. Absorbed from CL-ONT.
components.StrategicOpportunity: Identified strategic opportunity arising from market gaps. Absorbed from CL-ONT.
components.CompetitiveAnalysis: Formal competitive analysis record (SWOT, Porter's, benchmarking). Absorbed from CA-ONT.
components.CompetitorAssessment: Assessment of a specific competitor within a competitive analysis. Absorbed from CA-ONT.
components.MarketOffering: Products and services offered in the market by competitors. Absorbed from CA-ONT.
components.CompetitivePosition: Organization's market position relative to competitors. Absorbed from CA-ONT.
components.BlueOceanOpportunity: Uncontested market opportunity with minimal competition. Absorbed from CA-ONT.
components.AuthorityDomain: Domain authority classification linking organizations to their governance, regulatory, or standards domains. Added v3.1.0 for CG-ONT.

crossOntology.name: contextForOrganization
crossOntology.purpose: ORG → ORG-CONTEXT: Organizations receive rich context via bridge pattern

crossOntology.name: hasValueProposition
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → VSOM: Context references value propositions defined in VSOM

crossOntology.name: brandValueProposition
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → VSOM: Brand-level value proposition reference

crossOntology.name: productValueProposition
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → VSOM: Product-level value proposition reference

crossOntology.name: hasContextAssignments
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Context type assignments for multi-dimensional classification

crossOntology.name: maturityClassifiedByFunction
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Per-function maturity assessment (marketing, sales, ops, etc.)

crossOntology.name: maturityClassifiedByTech
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Technology maturity (cloud, digital, automation)

crossOntology.name: maturityClassifiedByAI
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: AI readiness (data, talent, deployment)

crossOntology.name: sizedByContext
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Multi-dimensional size (headcount, customers, revenue)

crossOntology.name: marketClassifiedBy
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Industry classification (SIC, NAICS, GICS)

crossOntology.name: hasCultureContext
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Culture dimensions (innovation, risk, collaboration)

crossOntology.name: hasGeoContext
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Geographic context (HQ, regions, regulatory jurisdictions)

crossOntology.name: hasMarketPositioning
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Market positioning (share, brand, differentiation)

crossOntology.name: competitorBridgedByContext
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Competitor threat assessment and recommendations

crossOntology.name: hasRCSGContext
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → RCSG-FW: RCSG requirements (regulatory, compliance, governance)

crossOntology.name: hasLocalizationContext
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → CTX: Localization (currency, tax, data residency, formatting)

crossOntology.name: brandHasDesignSystem
crossOntology.purpose: ORG-CONTEXT → DS: Brand-level design system correlation

versionHistory.changes: OAA v5 compliance, bridge pattern implementation, value proposition separation to VSOM

OAA v6.1.0 upgrade: 21 relationships (17 internal + 4 cross-ontology) forming single connected component, 6 business rules with IF-THEN format, crossOntologyReferences with keyBridges for ORG and VSOM bridges, all 12 entities fully connected

Added 13 cross-ontology bridges to ContextTypes (CTX) for multi-dimensional context classification: market, size, maturity (functional/tech/IT/AI), culture, geo, positioning, competitor, RCSG, and localization. Total relationships now 30 internal + 17 cross-ontology.
Retargeted hasRCSGContext bridge from ctx:RCSGContext to rcsg-fw:RCSGContext following RCSG framework entity extraction to RCSG-FW-ONT. Added Entry-ONT-RCSG-FW-001 to dependencies.

Added brandHasDesignSystem cross-ontology bridge from org-ctx:Brand to ds:DesignSystem for design system to organization correlation. DS-ONT added as dependency. Total relationships: 31 internal + 18 cross-ontology.

Epic 9C: Absorbed CL-ONT and CA-ONT entities into ORG-CONTEXT. 9 new entity types (CompetitorProfile, MarketDynamic, CompetitiveAdvantage, StrategicOpportunity, CompetitiveAnalysis, CompetitorAssessment, MarketOffering, CompetitivePosition, BlueOceanOpportunity) with 8 new internal relationships. Competitive series eliminated. Total: 21 entities, 39 internal + 18 cross-ontology relationships.

Added AuthorityDomain entity for domain authority classification (CyberSecurity, DataProtection, RiskManagement, ITGovernance, etc.) with hasAuthorityDomains relationship from OrganizationContext hub. Supports CG-ONT governance ontology domain authority modelling. Epic 30 F30.7 S30.7.2. Total: 22 entities, 40 internal + 18 cross-ontology relationships.

notes: Hub ontology implementing the bridge pattern to connect organizations to all domain contexts. Value proposition details live in VSOM. ContextTypes (CTX) provides the extensible typed context dimension taxonomy via 13 cross-ontology bridges. CL-ONT and CA-ONT entities absorbed as subtypes in v3.0.0. AuthorityDomain added v3.1.0 for governance domain authority classification.
